RiftBeacon.name: Rift Beacon
RiftBeacon.pickup:
Survive a deadly hit by tearing open a

rift.
RiftBeacon.description:
Dropping below 25% health grants a barrier for 25% of max health and opens a

rift for 8s: enemies within ~250 are periodically flung away. Kills during the rift extend it by 1s. Breaks after triggering and repairs each stage.

RiftBeaconDeactivate.name: Broken Rift Beacon
RiftBeaconDeactivate.pickup: Repairs at the start of the next stage.
RiftBeaconDeactivate.description:
Repairs into

Rift Beacon at the start of the next stage.

IngrownIdol.name: Ingrown Idol
IngrownIdol.pickup: Feeds on <r>kills</c> and, when sated, looses a <r>beast</c> to hunt for you.
IngrownIdol.description: Each <b>kill</c> feeds the idol: <y>1</c> normal, <y>2</c> elite, <y>3</c> boss. Every <b>30 offerings</c> the idol calls a <r>powerful beast</c> that hunts your enemies for <b>45s</c>, striking for <y>200% damage</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c>. After <b>20 minutes</c> offerings are halved (<y>0.5</c> / <y>1</c> / <y>2</c>).
IngrownIdol.story: It starts as a cold lump you can close a hand around. Feed it - it does not care whose blood, only how much - and it warms, settles in under the skin, and waits. When it has eaten its fill the carving splits and something steps out: not the idol, not you, but the thing the two of you make together, and it hunts the way the idol hungers. It runs itself down to nothing and folds back into the bone. The medics stopped asking what it is; they just tally what it leaves behind.

ContagionBarb.name: Contagion Barb
ContagionBarb.description: <b>15%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> chance on hit to cause <r>bleeding</c> for <r>40% damage per second</c> over <b>4s</c>. Killing a bleeding enemy <r>spreads</c> the bleed to <b>1</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> nearby enemy.

CharlatansDie.name: Charlatan's Die
CharlatansDie.pickup: The first chest each stage may drop a <b>better item</c>.
CharlatansDie.description: The <b>first chest</c> opened each stage has a <b>35%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> chance to also drop a higher-rarity item (<g>uncommon</c>, or <r>rare</c>).

StandingRequisition.name: Standing Requisition
StandingRequisition.pickup: Each stage delivers a <b>choice</c> of items.
StandingRequisition.description: At the start of each stage, a <b>delivery crate</c> appears nearby offering a <b>choice of 2</c> items - take one, the other is lost. Higher stacks improve the <b>rarity</c> odds.

LootFever.name: Loot Fever
LootFever.pickup: Picking up items <g>boosts your stats</c> for a while.
LootFever.description: Picking up any <b>item</c> grants a stack of <r>Fever</c>: <y>+3%</c> to <y>damage</c>, <y>attack speed</c>, <b>movement speed</c> and <g>regeneration</c> for <b>20s</c>. Stacks up to <b>4</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> times.

MagicMissile.name: Magic Missile
MagicMissile.pickup: Your attacks <y>loose darts that never miss</c>.
MagicMissile.description: Each attack spends a charge to fire a <y>dart</c> at the nearest enemy for <y>120% damage</c> <c_stack>(+60% per stack)</c>. Holds <b>4</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> charges, restoring <b>1</c> every <b>2.5s</c>.
MagicMissile.destination:
Hobby Deck 4,
Quartermaster Stores,
UES Safe Travels
MagicMissile.story: Recovered from a hobbyist's footlocker, wedged between a bag of dice and a worn rulebook. It is a wrist emitter that throws thin darts - no fins, no charge we can measure - and yet every dart finds the target, even when the shooter does not. Two words are scratched into the casing: MAGIC MISSILE. The one tech who pried a unit open reports it stopped curving its shots afterward, so we stopped prying them open. Issue as-is.

BountyContract.name: Bounty Contract
BountyContract.pickup: <r>Execute</c> weakened elites and earn <b>gold</c> for every elite slain.
BountyContract.description: Instantly <r>kills</c> <r>elites</c> (not bosses) below <b>12%</c> <c_stack>(+12% per stack, hyperbolic)</c> health. Every <b>elite kill</c> grants <b>$40</c> <c_stack>(+$20 per stack)</c>. Ordinary kills grant no gold. Gold scales with time.

Golemshot.name: Golemshot
Golemshot.description: Activating any <b>interactable</c> deploys a <y>golem turret</c> for <b>30s</c> that fires at the nearest enemy within <b>~250</c> every <b>0.75s</c> for <y>100% damage</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c>. Up to <b>1</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> golem at once.
Golemshot.story: The mining crews left them in the tunnels - squat ceramic constructs, knee-high, meant to guard a claim while the shift slept. They wake when someone works a mechanism nearby: a lever, a lock, a drill. Then the golem plants its feet, opens a port in its chest, and fires on anything not listed on the payroll. It cannot walk and it will not follow. Stand it beside the thing you are opening and it earns its keep for half a minute, then folds back into a rock you would step over without a second look.

BombardierSwarm.name: Bombardier Swarm
BombardierSwarm.pickup: Summons a <y>bombardier drone</c> that drops <r>dynamite</c> on enemies.
BombardierSwarm.description: Summons a <y>bombardier drone</c> that flies to the nearest enemy and every <b>10s</c> drops a <r>stick of dynamite</c>; each of your nearby <b>drones</c> drops one too. The dynamite falls under gravity and explodes for <r>250% damage</c> <c_stack>(+125% per stack)</c> within <b>~150</c>, <b>stunning</c> for <b>1s</c>.

RustMite.name: Rust Mite
RustMite.description: <y>100% chance</c> on hit to stack <r>Rust</c> on the target - each stack makes it take <r>+4%</c> more damage from <b>any source</c>, <r>permanently</c>.

MosquitoNet.name: Mosquito Net
MosquitoNet.pickup: <b>Shoot down</c> projectiles and periodically <b>block a hit</c>.
MosquitoNet.description: Every <b>0.5s</c>, shoot down <b>2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> <r>enemy projectiles</c> within <b>~12m</c> (recharge <b>scales with attack speed</c>). Every <b>7s</c> <c_stack>(-10% per stack, hyperbolic)</c>, fully <b>block</c> one incoming hit if it deals more than <b>10%</c> of current health.

OrbitalLens.name: Orbital Lens
OrbitalLens.pickup: Chance to call a <r>meteor</c> on hit.
OrbitalLens.description: <y>3%</c> <c_stack>(+2% per stack)</c> chance on hit to call a <r>meteor</c> for <y>800%</c> <c_stack>(+250% per stack)</c> base damage. The more <y>damage you deal</c>, the <y>higher the chance and meteor damage</c> (decays over time).

FaultyConductor.name: Faulty Conductor
FaultyConductor.description: Every <b>15s</c> <c_stack>(-20% per stack)</c>, release a wave that <y>energizes allies</c> for <b>7s</c>: <y>+30% attack and movement speed</c>. <b>Drones</c> permanently deal <y>+15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c> more damage.

RavenousVine.name: Ravenous Vine
RavenousVine.pickup: Nearby enemies <g>feed the vine</c>; when ripe it <y>blooms</c>: area damage and <y>healing</c>.
RavenousVine.description: Enemies within <b>5 tiles</c> <c_stack>(+1.5 per stack)</c>, up to <b>6</c> <c_stack>(+4 per stack)</c>, <g>feed the vine</c>: the more of them, the faster it ripens. When ripe, the vine deals <y>600%</c> <c_stack>(+300% per stack)</c> damage to all nearby enemies and heals <y>9% max HP</c> <c_stack>(+4.5% per stack)</c>.

CascadeFailure.name: Cascade Failure
CascadeFailure.description: <r>Infect</c> up to <b>4</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> enemies. <y>50%</c> of damage you deal to infected is <b>pooled</c>. Every <b>3s</c>, all infected take <y>100% of the pool</c>.

ArmySurplus.name: Army Surplus
ArmySurplus.pickup: Gain a <b>combat drone</c> and reinforce your <b>drones</c>.
ArmySurplus.description: Deploy a <b>combat drone</c> that fights on its own. All <b>drones</c> gain <y>+25%</c> <c_stack>(+25% per stack)</c> damage and <y>+15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c> max health.

CashbackChip.name: Cashback Chip
CashbackChip.description: Refunds <y>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack, hyperbolic)</c> of the <y>gold</c> spent on <b>any purchase</c>. Additionally, the <g>first chest</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> you buy each stage is <g>free</c>.
CashbackChip.story:
A slim card of brushed alloy, warm to the touch, with a single contact that never tarnishes. The traders on the outer ring passed it hand to hand without quite trusting it: every time coin left your account, a fraction crept quietly back, as if the chip skimmed the seller instead of you.

No one could say where the returned money came from. The ledgers always balanced - to the buyer's favor. The cashiers stopped checking and started smiling.

We filed it as 'loyalty instrument, anomalous yield.' To myself I noted: the house always wins, unless the house is in your pocket.

Nightshade.name: Nightshade
Nightshade.pickup: Summon a <r>shadow assassin</c> that blinks between enemies.
Nightshade.description: On use, summon a <r>shade</c> that <b>blinks</c> onto the nearest enemy, strikes for <r>133% damage</c>, then vanishes - repeating for <b>13 seconds</c>. It cannot be killed.
Nightshade.destination:
Containment Vat 7,
Symbiotics Lab,
UES PRIME
Nightshade.story:
It came in a sealed vat, a slick black mass that mirrored whatever stood before it. Set a fighter beside it and it learned his shape - then learned to step out of sight between one breath and the next.

We never saw it cross the room. It was here, then it was on the enemy, then it was nowhere, and the enemy was down.

We logged it as 'autonomous infiltration surrogate.' The crew named it for the quiet little flower that kills without a sound.

TacticalVisor.name: Tactical Visor
TacticalVisor.description: On use, gain <y>+100% Critical Chance</c> for <b>8 seconds</c>.
TacticalVisor.destination:
Optics Bay,
Marksman Range 3,
UES SAFE TRAVELS
TacticalVisor.story:
A targeting visor that paints the world in weak points. For a few seconds after you key it, every shot finds the seam, the joint, the soft place behind the plate.

The gunners said it didn't aim for you - it just stopped letting you miss the part that matters.

We wrote 'fire-control overlay, transient.' They wrote nothing; they were too busy hitting everything.

Hunger.name: Hunger
Hunger.pickup:
Open a

black hole that pulls enemies in.
Hunger.description:
On use, launch a

black hole that drifts forward and pulls enemies toward its center for 10 seconds. Deals no damage; bosses are immune.
Hunger.story:
A palm-sized cube that weighs nothing until you press it - then the air folds inward and everything not bolted down begins to lean toward a point that should not exist.

It takes no life of its own. It only gathers. Whatever it cannot move, it ignores; whatever it can, it brings together, patiently, into one helpless knot.

We filed it as 'localized gravity well.' The crew named it for the look it gave the horde: a quiet, bottomless appetite.

Annihilator.name: Annihilator
Annihilator.description: On use, fire a slow <b>charge</c> that zaps nearby enemies for up to <r>600% damage/second</c>. On contact it <r>detonates</c> in a massive <b>20m</c> blast for <r>4000% damage</c>.
Annihilator.destination:
Reactor Spine,
Weapons Test Annex,
UES PRIME
Annihilator.story:
A reactor's heart in a launch tube. The charge drifts forward slow enough to watch, and that is the cruel part - you see the tendrils reach out and start unmaking whatever they touch long before the end.

Then it meets something solid, and there is a light, and where the light was there is nothing.

We logged it as 'preon discharge unit, single use per cycle.' Everyone who saw it fire logged it as the last thing the target ever did.

FacetedJade.name: Faceted Jade
FacetedJade.description: After taking <r>no damage for 5s</c>, gain <y>+45 armor</c> <c_stack>(+45 per stack)</c>. The next hit you take consumes it until you stay safe again.
FacetedJade.story:
A cut of green jade with far too many faces for any jeweller's wheel to manage. Leave it on the bench and a glassy skin grows over it, slow, like frost on a window. Knock it - even lightly - and the skin goes all at once, with the sound of a dropped plate. Wait, and it starts over.

The appraiser sent it back with a note: «Can't price it. It's only ever armored when nobody's looking at it.»

ElephantGun.name: Elephant Gun
ElephantGun.description: Deal <y>+15%</c> <c_stack>(+15% per stack)</c> more damage and gain <y>+10% Critical Chance</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> against <r>bosses</c>.
ElephantGun.destination:
Big-Game Reserve,
Trophy Hold,
Savannah Belt, Earth
ElephantGun.story:
A rifle too heavy for any sane shoulder, bored wide enough to swallow a fist. It was never meant for small things - fire it at a scurrying target and you'll only deafen yourself. But aim it at something vast, something that thinks its size makes it safe, and the round goes in like a verdict.

The big-game hunter who carried it kept one rule in his log: «Never waste it on what you could simply outrun. Save it for what you cannot.»

PainTax.name: Pain Tax
PainTax.description: On taking <r>damage</c> from an enemy, gain <y>$8</c> <c_stack>(+8 per stack)</c>. <y>Increases over time</c>.
PainTax.story:
A black ledger-coin, warm to the touch, with a slot worn smooth on one face. Every time its bearer is wounded, the coin grows heavier by a few grams of gold - as if someone, somewhere, were settling an account in pain.

We never found the other party. The clerk who studied it longest began flinching at his own paperwork: «It pays well, but it only pays when it hurts - and the longer you carry it, the more it decides your suffering is worth.»

CheatDeath.name: Cheat Death
CheatDeath.description: <b>10%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> of <r>damage taken</c> is delayed as <r>bleed</c> over <b>5s</c> (capped at <b>100%</c>) - giving you time to <g>heal it off</c>.
CheatDeath.story:
A pair of skeletal hands, cupped like they're catching rain. There's always blood in them, welling up between the finger bones and running over, no matter how often you wipe them dry.

The man we took them from should have died on the table. Took a wound that empties a body in seconds - and just didn't. Bled slow instead. Slow enough to walk to the medbay on his own. He swore the hands held the worst of it back, doling it out a drop at a time so he'd have time to patch the rest.

He cheated death twice more before it stopped taking the joke.

Concentration.name: Concentration
Concentration.pickup: Being <g>out of danger</c> empowers your <y>next hit</c>.
Concentration.description: After taking <r>no damage for 5s</c>, your <y>next attack</c> deals <y>+60%</c> <c_stack>(+60% per stack)</c> bonus damage. Consumed on hit.
Concentration.story:
A plain metal cuff with a single green eye set into it. The eye sits half-shut. Stand still, stay out of trouble, and it opens - wider the longer you hold - until it's staring dead ahead at whatever you are. Move too soon and it droops closed again, bored.

Whatever it's looking at when it's fully open gets hit a lot harder than it should. The scouts who wore it stopped spraying and started waiting. «One good shot,» they'd say, «is the one the cuff was awake for.»

Tankbuster.name: Tankbuster
Tankbuster.pickup: Periodic <y>heavy hit</c> that scales with <r>enemy HP</c>.
Tankbuster.description: Every <b>7s</c>, your next hit deals an extra <y>+40%</c> <c_stack>(+20% per stack)</c> of your damage plus <y>2%</c> <c_stack>(+1% per stack)</c> of the target's <r>max HP</c>.
Tankbuster.destination:
Demolition Bay,
Sapper's Depot,
UES PRIME
Tankbuster.story:
A squat iron bomb with a single lidded eye bolted to the casing. It doesn't tick. It watches. Set it loose in a crowd and it ignores the small fry entirely - the eye rolls past them, hunting, and locks onto the biggest, fattest thing in the room.

Then it goes off against that one, and only that one. The engineers never got it to care about anything it judged beneath it. «It's not a bomb,» one wrote in the test log. «It's a grudge with a fuse.»

BountyTag.name: Bounty Tag
BountyTag.pickup: <y>Gold</c> per <r>kill</c>, more on a clean streak.
BountyTag.description: Gain <y>$2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> per <r>kill</c>, plus <y>$1</c> for each kill in a row <b>without taking damage</c> (up to <b>+$10</c>). Taking damage resets the streak. <y>Increases over time</c>.
BountyTag.destination:
Contracts Desk,
Bounty Office,
Edge Frontiers
BountyTag.story:
A demon's head, mounted and lacquered, the kind a certain sort of officer likes to hang over the desk. The tongue lolls. The eyes have been swapped for two little counters that tick over every time something dies nearby.

It pays out in hard coin - more for a clean run, less the moment you take a hit yourself. The collector who hung it swore it kept honest books. «It doesn't care who you kill,» he told us. «Only that nothing touched you while you did it.»

OneArmyMan.name: One Army Man
OneArmyMan.pickup: When it's you against the horde - <r>bring more horde</c>.
OneArmyMan.description: On use, <b>BFG starts playing</c> for <b>30s</c>. For the first <b>22s</c> gain <y>+50% attack speed</c> and <y>+50% damage</c>, and <g>heal 1.5% of max HP per kill</c>. For the <b>whole track</c> the <r>director floods the stage with enemies</c>, including <r>elites</c> - so the assault outlives your buffs. Enemies spawned during the song drop <r>no experience</c> and only <y>20% gold</c>.
OneArmyMan.story:
They are rage, brutal, without mercy. But you. You will be worse.

Rip and tear, until it is done.

The Slayer came not for glory, nor for gold, nor for any reward the realms could offer. He came because the legions of Hell were loosed, and someone had to meet them. Where he walks, the horde rises against him - and finds only its end. He does not count the dead. He does not mourn them. He moves to the next, and the next, and the killing does not stop while the war-hymn still plays.

Hell feared him. Hell named him the Doom Slayer. And against the dark he stands alone, and that is enough.

HeavyLungs.name: Heavy Lungs
HeavyLungs.description: Grants a <g>shield</c> equal to <g>5%</c> of your <g>maximum HP</c> for each <b>drone</c>. Increases <b>drone</c> <g>HP</c> and <g>regeneration</c> by <g>10%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c>.
HeavyLungs.destination:
Pulmonary Complex,
Life-Support Bay,
UES PRIME
HeavyLungs.story:
A pair of heavy breathing bellows, cast as if from lead - a chore to lift, yet almost silent at work. The engineers on the lower decks fitted them to old repair drones: a machine wearing them lasted longer and shrugged off overloads, as though it had learned to breathe deep and slow.

We tested them on our own tin cans. A drone with these lungs held under fire far more stubbornly, and around its owner a thin protective film seemed to settle - thicker the more machines circled nearby, as if they shared their own reserve of air with their master.

In the report I wrote 'pressurization circuit, improves bearer survivability'. To myself I thought otherwise: the one who breathes heavy is the one who is not alone.

LaughingFungus.name: Laughing Fungus
LaughingFungus.description: After <b>standing still for 1 second</c>, you create a zone that every second <g>heals</c> all <g>allies</c> within <b>3 meters</c> <c_stack>(+1.5m per stack)</c> for <g>6%</c> <c_stack>(+3% per stack)</c> of <g>your maximum health</c>.
LaughingFungus.story:
We brought back another curiosity from the gorge: a squat green mushroom that quivers when no one moves near it. Stand still beside it and it swells, releasing a fine, warm haze that settles into the lungs like a quiet chuckle. Cuts knit, fatigue lifts, and for some reason everyone in the haze starts to smile.

The lynx, it seems, gathered around such caps to rest between hunts - never while running, only while still. The moment you stir, the mushroom shrinks back into the soil, sulking, and the haze is gone.

In the report I wrote 'restorative spore emitter, requires immobility'. In my notes I added: it heals best those who finally learn to stop and breathe.

AssemblyBlueprint.name: Assembly Blueprint
AssemblyBlueprint.pickup: Chance to <b>duplicate purchased drones</c> and <g>rebuild dead ones</c>.
AssemblyBlueprint.description: <y>20%</c> chance <c_stack>(+20% per stack, hyperbolic, up to 80%)</c> to <b>duplicate a drone or turret on purchase</c>. Additionally, <g>10%</c> chance <c_stack>(+10% per stack, hyperbolic, up to 80%)</c> to <g>rebuild a drone or turret when it dies</c>, returning it at <g>50% HP</c>.
AssemblyBlueprint.destination:
Fabrication Line 7,
Drydock Assembly,
UES PRIME
AssemblyBlueprint.story:
A folded sheet of dark drafting film, edges worn soft from handling. Hold it to the light and the schematic on it seems to redraw itself - never quite the same machine twice, as if it were sketching whatever drone happened to be nearest.

The deck crews swore by it. Pay to raise one repair drone off the husk, and now and then a second would whir to life beside it, identical down to the dents, with no one having paid the difference. And when a machine was finally shot to scrap, the film would dim, and from the wreck the crews would haul a half-charged twin - battered, half-fueled, but flying.

I wrote it up as a 'self-replicating fabrication template'. Privately I thought the old shipwright's line: a good drawing never dies with the thing it built - it simply waits to be read again.

GoldenSneakers.name: Golden Sneakers
GoldenSneakers.pickup: Grants <b>movement speed</c> based on your current <y>gold</c>.
GoldenSneakers.description: Grants up to <b>+50% movement speed</c> based on your current <y>gold</c>. Reaches the cap at <y>700 gold</c> <c_stack>(-50% gold per stack)</c>. <y>Scales over time</c>.
GoldenSneakers.story:
A pair of sneakers plated in soft gold, light despite the metal. The courier who wore them swore he ran faster on payday than on any other morning - and slower the instant his pockets ran dry.

We laughed at first. Then we measured. The richer the wearer, the lighter his stride, as if the gold itself wanted to be carried somewhere, anywhere, fast. Spend it, and the shoes go heavy again, like ordinary leather.

We wrote it up as a 'motivational prototype'. Privately I think they simply love money more than their owner does - and they hurry to spend it on his behalf.

UnfocusPrisma.name: Unfocus Prism
UnfocusPrisma.description: Increases <y>damage</c> to enemies <b>more than 13 meters</c> away by <y>25%</c> <c_stack>(+25% per stack)</c>.
UnfocusPrisma.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
UnfocusPrisma.story:
Your first order is complete. A strange request - a lens that pulls focus off whatever is near and throws it into the distance. "Focus distortion," as you called it.

I ground the prism so that everything at arm's length blurs into a rainbow haze, while the far-off snaps sharp, as if dragged right up close. Looking down at your own feet through it is agony - but a target across the hall stands out to the last detail, and the hand strikes it dead-on, harder than it has any right to.

I don't understand why anyone would go blind up close for the sake of what lies far away. But you pay, and I don't ask needless questions. Still, I admit - I'm curious what you'll build from this, and from the lenses you'll order next.

ReserveCapacitor.name: Reserve Capacitor
ReserveCapacitor.description: Holds an <b>additional equipment charge</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c>. Each reserve charge recharges over <y>2x</c> the <b>equipment's cooldown</c>.
ReserveCapacitor.destination:
Reserve Armory,
Power-Cell Depot,
UES PRIME
ReserveCapacitor.story:
A small block with a matte casing and a single glowing contact. To the eye, just a spare power element, the kind we keep by the hundred in the hold.

But set it beside a piece of equipment and the gear seems to stop cooling down. We spent a charge, waited for the cooldown - and the device was ready again, as if a second mouthful of power had been tucked away for a rainy day. The engineers argued over where the extra surge came from; the instruments read exactly as much energy as we put in, not a joule more.

I wrote it up briefly: 'buffer cell, reduces downtime.' To myself I noted something else - it's reassuring to know you always have one more shot held in reserve.

SeaFossil.name: Sea Fossil
SeaFossil.description: Using equipment <g>Restores 120%</c> <c_stack>(+50% per stack)</c> <g>health</c> from cooldown time.
SeaFossil.story:
We found it at the edge of the shore, where sand no longer holds the step and the water drags you down. On the outside-an ordinary shell.

Put it to your ear and you won't hear the sea. Instead-a measured <b>hammer's beat</c>, as hollow as in the temple beneath the throne hall. Sometimes, slow, heavy steps too, as if someone still walks in circles, unaware all are gone. I recalled tales of the <r>'King without a kingdom'</c> and shuddered: can even stone and shell carry his echoes?

It grants <g>a drop of strength</c>, but seems to demand part of the will in return. The hand reaches for tools on its own, as in that cursed 'gesture', when the tool controls you more than you control it.

We didn't take it on the march. We placed it in a chest marked 'pseudo-strength'. Such things are easier left behind. Too often what looks like support is only a reminder that there are forces not to be envied-and they do not forgive curiosity.

Community.name: Community
Community.description: For each <b>item</c>, you gain <b>1%</c> <c_stack>(+1% per stack)</c> to all stats.
Community.destination:
Litovigla Cluster,
Multicut Mine,
Edge Frontiers
Community.story:
They say every world has its king.
We had a <r>TYRANT</c>, they had a <r>BULWARK</c>. Both held swords wider than a man, both believed they protected the weak from the strong.

But a throne is no fortress. A crown cannot save from the one who comes nameless and merciless. He comes to every world, tears each from the pedestal, and swords are equally dull before the one called hero.

We applaud when he topples the throne, and fall silent when he destroy the world.

Maybe heroes are not protectors, but simply <y>executioners</c> who recognize neither <r>throne</c> nor <g>gods</c>, trading a crown for the next victim. And if so-should one envy those called <g>king</c>?

GlassMagnifier.name: Glass Magnifier
GlassMagnifier.destination:
Archive 7,
Department of Optical Research,
UES PRIME
GlassMagnifier.story:
An old magnifier. The brass has darkened, but the glass is clean - as if polished only yesterday.

Look through it and you don't see farther, or larger. You see where it will break. The hairline crack beneath a rivet, the tired grain in a steel beam, the very spot where a thing is already ready to split at a touch. One of our men, studying a lathe through it, muttered: "It doesn't magnify. It passes sentence."

Later he stopped looking at machines. His last notes are about people: whose armor ran thin, whose hand wavered, whose - as he put it - "smile is hanging by a single thread." We put the magnifier away for good - after we found it aimed at a mirror, with him beside it.

ThinWings.name: Thin Wings
ThinWings.pickup: Reloads a <y>skill</c> and increases <b>movement speed</c> when interacting with an object.
ThinWings.description: When interacting with an object, increases <y>movement speed</c> by <b>15%</c><c_stack> (+8% per stack)</c>, and <b>reduces cooldown</c> of a random skill by <b>10%</c> for <b>10 seconds</c>.
ThinWings.story:
Large dragonfly wings, frozen in ice. Fragile to the eye, harder than bone to the touch, and plainly not made of chitin.

Fasten them to your back, and your hands reach for any mechanism on their own: for a lever, a latch, a humming box. They reach before you've decided to. By the end of the second week the testers no longer said "I opened it." They said "it opened."

MassacreCurse.name: Massacre Curse
MassacreCurse.description: Killing an <y>enemy</c> grants <y>1 stack</c><r> of 'Massacre'</c> for <y>2.5</c> seconds <c_stack>(+2.5 seconds)</c>. Increases <y>damage</c> and <g>armor</c> by <y>3%</c>.
MassacreCurse.story:
Another find from the same site. A small skull with a spine attached. Its color is strange: the bone seems to have absorbed <r>blood</c>. We even tried to wash it clean, but the color remained. At times, a white glow flickers in the sockets.

The soldiers say the artifact responds to killing. The more corpses around, the heavier it feels in hand, yet the stronger its bearer becomes. Sounds like nonsense, but we counted far too many bones near its previous owner.

MagmaFragment.name: Magma Fragment
MagmaFragment.description: <y>5%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> <y>chance</c> to <r>ignite</c> <y>enemies</c>, dealing <y>30% TOTAL damage</c> over <b>5 seconds</c>. Only <b>one burn</c> can be active on an enemy at a time.
MagmaFragment.story:
Found it in the ashes of the east wing, still warm three days after the fire. Doesn't cool. Doesn't go out. You can hold it for about a second before it bites.

The investigators ruled the blaze an accident. They didn't find the letters, or what was left of them. Good.

This time, she definitely won't find out.

IceMixture.name: Ice Mixture
IceMixture.description: <y>7%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> <y>chance</c> to <b>slow movement speed</c> and <y>attack speed</c> of enemies by 30% for <b>3 seconds</c>.
IceMixture.story:
The first sample from the cryo program. We compressed dozens of substances, chilled them to the limit and let them fuse into a single crystal. It came out beautiful - blue, full of sparks.

The cold off it leaves a burn on the skin, and the living near it seem to forget how to hurry: blood thickens, breath draws out. But the limit we were after we never reached - fell the smallest bit short of true zero. The sample is unstable, and that "smallest bit" gave us pause: on the last test the crystal fell wholly silent, and with it the clock on the wall.

This batch we write off as scrap. But the direction is right - the boys are already preparing the next crystal, and it will come far nearer to absolute zero than ours. If it works, it will overturn everything we knew about superconductors. A pity only that the one who began it won't be the one to test the perfected thing.

GearFeather.name: Gear Feather
GearFeather.story:
First there was the <g>Hopoo Feather</c> - light, rare, almost mythical. Selling it was our first mistake: we lost more than we gained.

Now another feather is in our hands - <g>Gear</c>. Larger, heavier, yet it still pulls you upward when you leap. Outwardly similar, but inside it feels alien. Not a gift, but a bargain.

I wrote in the report: perhaps the first sale was a mistake. I don't want the second mistake to be <y>a dead end at the steam valve</c>.

Artifacts change owners, change worlds. Sometimes they don't break immediately - but they do break nonetheless.

FearEyes.name: Fear Eyes
FearEyes.pickup: Eyes are afraid, but hands still do the work.
FearEyes.description: Increases <y>damage by 15</c><c_stack>(+10 per stack)</c>. Breaks when <r>health drops</c> below <b>40%</c>.
FearEyes.story:
At first, I thought it was just a fish. Yes, fugu - I'd heard of those. But the eyes... they seemed to bulge out and freeze, as if it had seen something better left unseen. I placed it on the table - and it felt like its gaze was still drilling into me.

I don't know if it can be cooked, it's too strange.

I couldn't throw it away. Now it just lies in a container. Staring...

ShadowShield.name: Shadow Shield
ShadowShield.pickup: If you gaze long into the abyss, the abyss will begin to gaze into you.
ShadowShield.description: Upon entering a new stage, grants <b>invulnerability for 30</c><c_stack>(+10 per stack)</c> <b>seconds</c>, increases <b>movement speed by 20%</c><c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c>, and <b>armor by 40</c><c_stack>(+20 per stack)</c>. If the player <y>attacks</c>, the effect dissipates.
ShadowShield.story:
The full moon is the Shield of Faith:
As long as it shall rise,
I know that Mystery comes again,
That Wonder never dies.

I know that Shadow has its place,
That Noon is not our goal,
That Heaven has non-official hours
To soothe and mend the soul;

That witchcraft can be angel-craft
And wizard deeds sublime;
That utmost darkness bears a flower,
Though long the budding-time.

Rhinoplasty.name: Rhinoplasty
Rhinoplasty.pickup: What does a surgeon do with the leftover <g>skin</c>?
Rhinoplasty.description: Killing every 4 enemies grants 1 stack of <g>'skin'</c>. After <b>10</c> seconds all <g>'skin pieces'</c> will be absorbed, giving you <g>40</c> <c_stack>(+20 per stack)</c> <g>health</c> for each <g>'skin'</c> stack.
Rhinoplasty.story:
Attention everyone!
Due to the development of new tools, we need to replace the surgical kits!
Each kit must now include the following: scalpels, clamps, sutures, sterile masks, anesthesia capsules, blood reserves, tissue implant cards, and an auto-injector for coagulation.

And also - a new prototype knife. The blade is made of green medical metal, with a twisted vessel running along it. Upon contact, it takes part of the skin from the wound and returns it to the body. I don't know whether to call this progress, but the scientists insist these tools be kept locked away. Just in case they decide to work without a patient.

GreenTea.name: Green Tea
GreenTea.description: Increases <g>health regeneration</c> by <g>0.546</c> <c_stack>(+0.546 per stack)</c> <g>units</c>. Increases <g>maximum health pool</c> by <g>6.8%</c> <c_stack>(+6.8% per stack)</c>.
GreenTea.destination:
Sheridan S.
Northlake,
UNITED STATES OF AMERICA,
Mars
GreenTea.story:
Mixed up a trial batch: an extract from that giant ginseng you sent for your cancer research, plus a drop of that healing gel from the vial - you know the one, with the whole laundry list of side effects down the label. Brewed some ordinary green tea over the top, for taste. Came out interesting.

Apart, one's a dubious little root and the other's poison with healing thrown in. Together, and watered down at that, they behave themselves: cuts close faster, breathing steadies, strength picks up. No rash, no itch - as if I took half of each and left the worse half in the glass. Worst of all, it tastes good.

That was two weeks ago. I've kept brewing it since. I tell myself it's for the notes. I drink it when there's nothing left to heal. Try it and tell me straight whether it's time to quit - because asking myself no longer works.

Accountant.name: Accountant
Accountant.description: Activating equipment grants you <y>+50% attack speed</c> for <y>6 seconds</c> <c_stack>(+3 seconds per stack)</c>.
Accountant.story:
A transparent vessel, inside - a brain suspended in viscous fluid. Cables run into the wall, carrying a continuous stream of data. We use it for resource accounting, tax calculations, and supply forecasts.

Once, this was someone's brain. The name erased, the past erased - only the function remains: to calculate, store, and process. And it does so flawlessly, more precise than any machine. But sometimes, in rare glitches, words appear on the screen that shouldn't exist: "I'm cold" or "Where are my hands."

Officially, it is an accounting supercomputer. Unofficially, it is a reminder that for every perfect calculation, someone has already paid the price.

BloodyRelic.name: Bloody Relic
BloodyRelic.description: Dealing damage has a <y>10%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> chance to grant <r>bloodlust</c>, which increases <y>attack speed</c> by <y>5%</c> for <y>8 seconds</c>. Effect can stack up to <b>5</c> <c_stack>(+1 per stack)</c> <b>times</c>.
BloodyRelic.story:
We thought the pauldron and the skull were the limit of what twisted rebel minds could create. But under a pile of rusted blades, we found a necklace. The stone in the center looks like an eye, and it's <r>red</c>, as if filled with fresh blood. It stares straight at you, even when you look away.

Rumors spread quickly among the workers: the relic gives "thirst" - the more blood you spill, the faster you move, the more eagerly your hands raise weapons. One guard held it, and now he utters no sound, his gaze darting as though he hears the heartbeat of those who can still be killed. They shackled him and took him to the med bay.

In short - don't remove it from the container, don't let anyone touch it without proper gear, etc., etc., standard safety. I don't need to explain that to you.

GoldBar.name: Gold Bar
GoldBar.pickup: Grants <y>gold</c> at the start of a stage.
GoldBar.description: Upon entering a stage grants <y>$33</c> <c_stack>(+22 per stack)</c>. <y>Increases over time</c>.
GoldBar.story:
Pulled from an old safe during the dig at the estate of some long-vanished rich men. To look at it, an ordinary gold bar. But marks are etched along its edge, like figures. The jewelers agreed to a man: it's no stamp and no hallmark - the metal seems to have always borne them.

The figures won't hold still. The cashier who held the bar swore the number crept slowly upward, right there in his hand, and the longer you held it the heavier it pressed on your palm. He set it down when he understood - that isn't a price climbing. That's a debt.

Take it into safekeeping. Paper burns, accounts reset to zero, but gold stays gold. I only rest easier when this bar is lying in someone else's hands.

GiftPurity.name: Gift of Purity
GiftPurity.pickup: <b>The third skill recharges faster</c> and <g>grants barrier</c>
GiftPurity.description: <b>Reduces cooldown</c> of the third skill by <b>15%</c><c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c>. Using the <b>third skill</c> grants a temporary <g>barrier</c> for <g>1.5%</c><c_stack>(+0.5% per stack)</c> of your <g>maximum health</c> per second of the skill's base cooldown.
GiftPurity.destination:
Sanctuary of the Seer,
Hall Beneath the Moonlit Dome,
[REDACTED]
GiftPurity.story:
The Gift of Purity is the rarest sign of mercy. Those who receive it find in their hands a pair of smooth, <y>violet</c> berries, exuding a faint scent of the night air. They are not harvested, not cultivated, not passed from hand to hand. It is believed they can only be received by the will of the Seer - and only by those who have proven their devotion.

Unlike the beads that bring anxiety and death, the Gift of Purity brings silence and confidence. Those fortunate enough to carry it said that enemies seemed to hesitate, blows lost their sharpness, and the heart grew calm. No voices, no whispers, no laughter. Only the sense that an unseen hand stands nearby, and your life - is no longer your own.

Yet this is no simple gift. The berries are a symbol. While they are fresh, you are under protection, but betrayal turns them into poison. Stories tell of deserters who found only shriveled fruits in their palms, bitter and deadly to the taste. The Seer, it is said, does not forgive those who break their oath.

Still, for communities that endured trials, the appearance of the Gift is a blessing. It reminds: the Seer not only punishes, but also protects. Where beads carry terror and dread, the berries of purity bring hope and peace. Their rarity makes them elusive, but all who have heard of them know: it is a promise. A promise that loyalty will be noticed, and the faithful - never forgotten.

GravityLoop.name: Gravity Loop
GravityLoop.description: When <r>health drops</c> below <b>35%</c>, <g>restores 50% health</c>. Breaks after use.
GravityLoop.destination:
Biocollection No.3,
Greenhouse and Aquarium,
New-Gravenkamp, Earth
GravityLoop.story:
Good day.
I dug this trinket up at a mountain excavation. It lay beside a skeleton that a collapse like that should have ground to dust - yet the bones were whole, not a crack on them. The device, by contrast, is all cracks, fit to crumble at a breath.

Through my own carelessness I tripped and dropped it onto the rocks. I thought the find was done for. But neither it nor I took any harm: it was as though it drank the whole blow itself. Once.

Later I looked up the histories of its former owners. Every one was found like that first skeleton: not a scratch on the body, beside them the same dead box, burned out from within. Each survived the thing that should have killed him - and soon died of another: one finished off by hunger, one by the cold. I'm sending it all the same: beyond its age, the personal-protection market will pay well for this. Only warn the buyer honestly - it will save you once, and no more.

Obereg.name: Obereg
Obereg.description: When <r>health</c> falls below <r>50%</c>, increases <y>armor</c> by <b>30</c> <c_stack>(+30 per stack)</c>.
Obereg.destination:
Old mound near the Black Forest,
A village erased from the map,
[REDACTED]
Obereg.story:
When the enemy warbands came, the people had neither walls nor an army. An old woman brought forth a green star, forged from strange metal, from her oven and placed it on the threshold. "Let the house at least stand, while we yet live," she said.

The warriors fell one by one, and only when the snow was soaked with blood did the amulet begin to glow. It held their souls to their bodies, forcing them to rise so long as hands could still grasp an axe. But its light was cold and cruel: the brighter it burned, the faster life slipped away. Since then, it was said - this metal does not forgive the weak, it only delays death.

When the mound was covered in grass, only the star remained. Heavy, cold, with a rusty glint of alien blood.

ShieldGenerator.name: Shield Generator
ShieldGenerator.description: <b>Grants a shield equal to 10%</c> <c_stack>(+5% per stack)</c> of <b>maximum health</c>.
ShieldGenerator.destination:
Energy Bay,
Field Testing Department,
Station "Asterion"
ShieldGenerator.story:
Item: wearable shield-field generator. Housing - a cast block, an active core.

At rest - a steady blue glow, a faint hiss. Under load the sensors register a sharp spike in the field. Mounting is standard, onto armor.

The field absorbs an incoming blow in part; the remainder falls on the wearer. Beneath the field one hears one's own sounds returned - an incidental acoustic effect, no bearing on function.

Noted over the trial period: wearers under the field more often take unjustified risks. No restrictions on issue.

EyeDiamond.name: Diamond Eye
EyeDiamond.description: Increases <y>critical hit chance</c> by <y>10%</c> and <r>critical hit damage</c> by <r>150%</c> <c_stack>(+100% per stack)</c>.
EyeDiamond.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
EyeDiamond.story:
Your order has been processed. The frame is made of synthetic diamond, the lenses tinted <r>red</c>. The right lens has an integrated targeting module - it activates automatically, creating a glow visible even through fog.

I must warn you: these materials are far too expensive for a mere accessory. Considering your past orders, one gets the impression you're not looking for optics, but for a weapon. Please - if you are working for [REDACTED], be cautious. These glasses were made to <r>cut</c> more precisely, not to see further.

AtGMissileMk0.name: AtG Missile Mk. 0
AtGMissileMk0.description: On hit, you have a <y>7% chance</c> <c_stack>(+7% per stack)</c> to launch a missile dealing <y>75% TOTAL damage</c>.
AtGMissileMk0.destination:
Fort Joseph,
Belt No.1053,
[REDACTED],
Boron System
AtGMissileMk0.story:
Delivery of the prototype Viper Missile System.

Single-barrel configuration, extremely unreliable ignition system. Missiles often misfire or trigger prematurely, yet command insisted on field testing. Ammunition limited to a single launch, recommended for training purposes only.

Do not issue to regular units. According to engineers, the launcher poses a greater threat to the operator than to the target.

SporeOBloom.name: Spore O bloom
SporeOBloom.description:
On killing an enemy, has a 44% chance to release a

poison cloud dealing 60% (+60% per stack) damage over 5 seconds.
SporeOBloom.destination:
Hidden Nook,
Mountain of Creation,
Venus
SporeOBloom.story:
A funny thing happened once - in a rush I forgot a piece of bread inside my bag. Later, when I noticed a strange smell and opened it, the bread was already covered in moss, with a tiny purple mushroom growing nearby.
I carefully placed it in a jar with moist moss. Mushrooms, as I know, feed on moisture and decaying matter. So I spray it with water and give it small pieces of bread. It doesn't need green light or fertilizer, it just draws in dampness and absorbs the bread, releasing spores in the process. It even reacts to words - if you whisper something, it slightly raises its cap, as if listening. The main thing is not to let the jar dry out, and make sure the moss stays damp.
I'm sending it to you - best not to unscrew the lid indoors or place it near a fan. Once, after a draft, spores swirled everywhere inside and it hid in the moss for half a day. It seems to prefer twilight and being spoken to in a soft voice.

NewtonsMechanism.name: Newton's Mechanism
NewtonsMechanism.pickup: <b>Electricity</c> is not a toy for children.
NewtonsMechanism.description: Grants a <g>shield</c> for <g>4%</c> of your <g>max health</c>. While you have a shield, increases <g>armor by 30</c> <c_stack>(+30 per stack)</c>. When your <b>shield breaks</c>, release a <y>stunning pulse</c> for <y>150% damage</c> plus <y>130%</c> <c_stack>(+10% per stack)</c> of your <g>max shield</c>.
NewtonsMechanism.story: Hey, sending you this exhibit for your Newton memorial museum. I think it would fit Mr. Newton himself perfectly, heh! Just make sure when unpacking it you handle it with the utmost care, and once you place it in the museum, keep it in an impact-resistant container filled with that cushioning fluid we talked about earlier. Still, I advise keeping it at least 2 meters away - you wouldn't want your visitors turning into toasted pancakes, right?

CoolGlasses.name: Cool Glasses
CoolGlasses.description: Increases <y>critical hit chance by 5%</c>. On a <y>critical hit</c> creates a <r>solar strike</c>, dealing <y>20%</c> <c_stack>(+20% per stack)</c> <y>TOTAL damage</c> in a <b>2m</c> radius.
CoolGlasses.destination:
Gravenkamp,
5th Houston Street,
Prism Tower,
Earth
CoolGlasses.story:
A simple frame, dark lenses, nothing remarkable at first glance. But under stress they overheat and emit a glow like a small <y>sun</c>. With your hands, the effect becomes even more dangerous.

I don't understand why you need three different sets at once. First you asked for focus distortion, then diamond lenses, now a black sun. If these are all parts of one project, you're playing with something that could easily spiral out of control.

Remember: even the 'coolest' glasses still break if you throw them.

DeepcoreGK2.name: Deepcore GK2
DeepcoreGK2.description: Gives <y>'Bosco'</c>, which periodically fires at enemies, dealing <y>3% damage</c> and <b>slowing</c> them. Each <b>new enemy</c> hit increases <b>movement speed</c> by <b>0.7%</c> until the end of the stage. Number of targets: <y>2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c>, target range: <b>10m</c> <c_stack>(+1m per stack)</c>. Bosco's fire rate depends on <y>attack speed</c>.
DeepcoreGK2.story: Yo, thanks for the drone parts - they fit my boys like a glove. In return I'm sending you a pretty rare piece - not exactly contraband, but getting it was a real pain. A GK-series assault rifle and a (slightly worn) Autonomous Support Drone (A.S.D.) - exclusive gear from the military-mining company in the Hoxxes system! You'll figure out the rifle yourself, you're no fool, but about the drone - damn, even worn-out, I'd have kept it for myself. Most of its functions are gone, but it still has its laser! Can't say for sure, but it's a patented technology that allows it to remain functional almost indefinitely without losing combat potential. Just make sure no one finds out you've got it - the dwarf who sold it to me went through hell to get it.

Totemetry.name: Totemetry
Totemetry.pickup: Periodically creates a totem that increases <y>attack speed</c> and <g>heals</c> in a large radius.
Totemetry.description: Every <b>2 minutes</c> creates a totem lasting <b>45 seconds</c>, which <g>heals allies</c> in a <y>7.5m</c> radius <c_stack>(+2m per stack)</c> for <g>2%</c> <c_stack>(+2% per stack)</c> of their <g>maximum health</c> every <b>3 seconds</c>. The appearance of the totem increases <y>attack speed</c> by <y>10%</c>.
Totemetry.destination:
Excavations in the Fungal Gorge,
Observation Sector,
[REDACTED]
Totemetry.story:
We found it among the debris - a green rectangular stone, as if carved by hands that never existed here. At the first touch it trembled, and nearby a <g>totem</c> appeared, resembling the structures raised by lynxes. It glowed with a soft light, and everyone within range felt lighter, their breathing steadier.

It seems this artifact is not merely an ornament, but a portable shrine. The lynx tribe uses it to gather together: it quickens their rhythm, lifts their spirit, heals their wounds. Stranger still - the stone activates on its own, as though deciding itself when its bearer needs to recover.

In it echo the songs of the lynx, their laughter and their dances. As if each activation brings back a piece of the tribe, even if they have long since returned to the earth. I do not know whose voice will answer the next time it shines.

RadialAlloy.name: Radial Alloy
RadialAlloy.description: After <r>taking damage</c>, <g>heals</c> for <g>10</c> <c_stack>(+5 per stack)</c> <g>HP</c>. <g>Healing</c> can offset incoming <r>damage</c> no lower than <b>5 HP</c>.
RadialAlloy.destination:
Materials Science Laboratory,
Research Sector No.12,
Mars
RadialAlloy.story:
Shards of metal, cut as if at a slant - faceted, as though by a beam. They tremble faintly in the hand.

Noted: if a wounded man holds them, the wound closes noticeably faster, as though the metal shares its strength with the body. By structure - an ordinary crystalline lattice, nothing special. By behavior - something else.

It doesn't take the pain away. It takes the pain for itself. Whoever carried a handful of the shards too long began complaining of wounds not his own: an aching break he never suffered, a burn from a fire long gone cold. In the report we put it briefly - "material with a healing effect." Among ourselves we argue about something else: where does all it has gathered go, once there is no room left inside it?

Maslenitsa.name: Maslenitsa
Maslenitsa.pickup: Increases <g>armor</c>. Grants <g>armor</c> for items that provide <g>armor</c>.
Maslenitsa.description: Increases <g>armor by 25</c> <c_stack>(+25 per stack)</c>. Additionally grants <g>2</c> <c_stack>(+2 per stack)</c> <g>armor</c> for each of: 'Hard Times', 'Reflective Armor', 'Huge Cone', 'Amulet', 'Shadow Shield'.
Maslenitsa.destination:
Hill with a burned village,
Abandoned shrine,
[REDACTED]
Maslenitsa.story:
When winter dragged on for a third year, people gathered on the hill and lit a great fire. Not for celebration - but for the final rite. They placed a green helmet, hammered with the sun itself, upon the head of the strongest warrior.

He stood, heavy as the earth itself, and the enemies could not break his armor. But each strike tore breath from his chest, and the helmet pressed him ever closer to the ground. People cried, sang, and threw pancakes into the fire, hoping to appease the sun. But spring never came.

The helmet was returned to the shrine after the warrior fell and never rose again. Now it lies in silence, the metal still bearing the gleam of those fires that warmed no one.

ShineOSun.name: Shine, O Sun
ShineOSun.pickup: Chance to create a <y>sun</c> when killing enemies.
ShineOSun.description: On killing an enemy, has a <y>22%</c> chance to create a <y>scorching sun</c>, dealing <r>5% of the enemy's maximum health</c> within a <b>12m</c> radius <c_stack>(+2.5m per stack)</c> for 5 seconds.
ShineOSun.destination:
Hidden Nook,
Mountain of Creation,
Venus
ShineOSun.story:
Guys, remember the little mushroom I sent yesterday? Today he has a new neighbor in the jar.
A tiny sun with little hands. I'm not joking - it's warm, like a cup of tea. When I made it laugh, its temperature rose and it melted the label on the jar. It doesn't ask for food either - it feeds on sunlight (though it is itself like a little sun). Caution: don't place near heaters or shake it - the glass already cracked a couple of times from the heat.
If it's awake, at night its glow can light the whole apartment, so keep it under a blanket or in a darkened box. In the evening I had to wrap the jar in a towel. Sometimes when I tapped the glass, it would peek out, like from behind a curtain. I'm sending it to you - I hope you have a night lamp companion, so it won't feel lonely.

Dombra.name: Dombra
Dombra.pickup: Chance to summon <r>berkut wings</c> on hit.
Dombra.description: On hit, you have a <y>20% chance</c> to summon <r>berkut wings</c> to your <b>left and right</c>, together dealing <y>122% TOTAL damage</c> <c_stack>(+122% per stack)</c>.
Dombra.story:
An old dombra, the wood darkened with age, two strings still taut. When its owner struck a kuy, those nearby swore they heard the beat of great wings overhead.

We set it down and plucked a string ourselves. Nothing - until a blow was struck in anger. Then the air to either side folded, and for an instant a pair of vast berkut wings unfurled, sweeping all before them, before melting into the wind.

The nomads say the steppe gives its strength to those who keep its songs. We wrote 'resonant melee amplifier' in the report. The herdsman who handed it over only smiled: play it well, and you never ride alone.

VioletFrenzy.name: Violet Frenzy
VioletFrenzy.pickup:
Killing an elite sends you into a

frenzy with near-instant cooldowns.
VioletFrenzy.description:
After killing an elite monster, enter a

frenzy for 4s (+4s per stack), during which all skill cooldowns are 0.5s.
VioletFrenzy.story: Recovered from a containment cell, sealed in a violet resin block. The handlers' log is mostly redacted; what remains describes a parasitic growth that fused to the host's spine and 'rewarded' kills with a flood of clarity - the world slowing, every motion arriving early. Test subjects reported the colour first: a faint lilac at the edges of sight that bloomed brighter the longer the hunt lasted. The final entry is a single line, underlined twice: it only quiets down while something is still dying.

DeadWater.name: Dead Water
DeadWater.pickup:
Healing is amplified and stored as

soul energy that strikes with skulls.
DeadWater.description:
Healing is increased by 75% (+50% per stack). 100% (+75% per stack) of healing is stored as

soul energy. When

soul energy reaches 10% of your maximum health, a skull is released, dealing 150% of the stored

soul energy.
DeadWater.destination:
Abandoned graveyard,
The well by the three aspens,
[REDACTED]
DeadWater.story:
From the well by the three aspens we drew a flask of murky, heavy water. The old folk called such water 'dead' - not for drinking, but for wounds. Splash it on a gash and the edges draw together on their own, the bleeding stills, the flesh knits, as though no blow had ever landed.

But the water takes its due. What it has healed does not vanish without trace - the pain it took gathers somewhere within it as a cold, dull glow. And when the vessel brims, that glow tears free in a grinning skull and seeks out whoever stands nearest. In the old tales dead water was always kept paired with living water: one mended the body, the other called back the spirit. We have no living water. Only the kind that mends the dead - and remembers every wound it ever closed.

In the report I wrote 'regenerative reagent with delayed discharge.' To myself I put it shorter: it heals you gladly, then collects the price from whomever it points to.
